title: Thelypteris friesii
intro: Thelypteris friesii, also known as Fries' maiden fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, pain, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris friesii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas and infusions, which can be used to treat colds, fever, and other respiratory infections.
